title: Onagawa Trawl Survey – Aug 2023 (Week 1)
text: Trawl survey summary (mock). Total catch counts: Japanese anchovy (Engraulis japonicus)=520, Chub mackerel (Scomber japonicus)=340, Japanese sardine (Sardinops melanostictus)=210. Notes: surface schooling fish were dominant; visibility moderate; light drizzle intermittently.

title: Onagawa Trawl Survey – Aug 2023 (Week 3)
text: Trawl survey summary (mock). Total catch counts: Japanese anchovy (Engraulis japonicus)=610, Chub mackerel (Scomber japonicus)=290, Pacific saury (Cololabis saira)=95. Notes: anchovy schools observed nearshore; sea state calm.

title: Onagawa eDNA Snapshot – Aug 2022
text: eDNA metabarcoding summary (mock). Relative read abundance: Engraulis japonicus high, Scomber japonicus medium, Sebastes spp low. Notes: eDNA read abundance is not a direct proxy for biomass; interpret cautiously.

title: Onagawa Environmental Log – Aug 2023
text: Environmental log (mock). Weather: occasional light drizzle; visibility variable. Sea surface temperature: 22-25C. Wind: low to moderate.

title: Onagawa Trawl Survey – Sep 2023 (Week 2)
text: Trawl survey summary (mock). Total catch counts: Japanese anchovy (Engraulis japonicus)=430, Chub mackerel (Scomber japonicus)=510, Japanese sardine (Sardinops melanostictus)=260, Japanese jack mackerel (Trachurus japonicus)=155. Notes: mackerel dominated offshore hauls; moderate swell; turbidity increased after rain.

title: Onagawa Trawl Survey – Oct 2023 (Week 1)
text: Trawl survey summary (mock). Total catch counts: Pacific saury (Cololabis saira)=180, Chub mackerel (Scomber japonicus)=240, Japanese anchovy (Engraulis japonicus)=95. Size notes: saury fork length 22–28 cm (n=60 subsample). Sea state: calm; visibility good.

title: Onagawa Set Net CPUE Log – Aug 2023 (Daily)
text: Set net log (mock). CPUE (kg/net/day): Japanese amberjack (Seriola quinqueradiata)=38.5, Japanese sardine (Sardinops melanostictus)=112.0, Japanese jack mackerel (Trachurus japonicus)=44.2. Notes: dawn peak activity; sardine school passed through 05:10–06:00.

title: Onagawa Acoustic Transect – Aug 2023 (Line A)
text: Acoustic survey (mock, 38 kHz). NASC index (m^2/nmi^2): pelagic layer=820, midwater=260. Species ground-truth (paired trawl): Japanese anchovy (Engraulis japonicus) dominant; Chub mackerel (Scomber japonicus) secondary. Notes: strong scattering at 10–25 m depth.

title: Onagawa eDNA Snapshot – Oct 2023
text: eDNA metabarcoding summary (mock). Relative read abundance: Cololabis saira high, Scomber japonicus medium, Engraulis japonicus medium, Sebastes spp low. Notes: reads suggest seasonal shift toward saury; treat as presence/relative signal only.

title: Onagawa Juvenile Beach Seine – Jul 2023 (Nursery)
text: Beach seine nursery survey (mock). Juvenile counts: Japanese flounder (Paralichthys olivaceus)=48, Black sea bream (Acanthopagrus schlegelii)=62, Japanese anchovy (Engraulis japonicus)=310. Notes: shallow eelgrass edge; water temp 23.4C; juveniles concentrated near structure.

title: Onagawa Bottom Trawl – Nov 2023 (Demersal)
text: Bottom trawl (mock). Total catch counts: Walleye pollock (Gadus chalcogrammus)=140, Pacific cod (Gadus macrocephalus)=28, Olive flounder (Paralichthys olivaceus)=19, Rockfish (Sebastes spp)=34. Notes: demersal assemblage increased; bottom temp 11.2C; oxygen adequate.

title: Onagawa Environmental Log – Nov 2023 (Fish-Relevant)
text: Environmental log (mock). SST: 14–16C; bottom temp: 10–12C; wind: moderate northerly. Notes: cooling correlated with reduced anchovy sightings and higher demersal catch share.

title: Sendai Bay Purse Seine Summary – Sep 2023
text: Purse seine haul summary (mock). Total catch (kg): Japanese sardine (Sardinops melanostictus)=8,900, Chub mackerel (Scomber japonicus)=2,150, Japanese anchovy (Engraulis japonicus)=760. Notes: sardine dominated; schooling observed 2–5 km offshore; calm seas.

title: Ishinomaki Fish Market Landings – Aug 2023 (Weekly)
text: Market landing report (mock). Landed weight (t): Scomber japonicus=41.2, Sardinops melanostictus=33.8, Engraulis japonicus=12.4, Cololabis saira=6.1. Notes: higher mackerel landings than prior week; average unit price increased for saury due to size grade.

title: Kesennuma Longline Log – Oct 2023
text: Pelagic longline log (mock). Hooked counts: Albacore tuna (Thunnus alalunga)=24, Skipjack tuna (Katsuwonus pelamis)=57, Blue shark (Prionace glauca)=4 (released). Notes: target tuna depth 60–120 m; sea surface temp 20.1C.

title: Sanriku Coastal Gillnet – May 2023
text: Gillnet effort summary (mock). CPUE (fish/100 m net/night): Pacific cod (Gadus macrocephalus)=3.2, Rockfish (Sebastes spp)=5.7, Japanese seabass (Lateolabrax japonicus)=1.1. Notes: higher rockfish near rocky habitat; minor net damage from swell.

title: Hokkaido Coastal Survey – Feb 2024 (Cold Season)
text: Coastal survey (mock). Total catch counts: Japanese sand lance (Ammodytes personatus)=1,240, Pacific herring (Clupea pallasii)=310, Walleye pollock (Gadus chalcogrammus)=95. Notes: sand lance schooling near sandy bottom; water temp 2.6C; icy wind.

title: Tokyo Bay Estuarine Seine – Jun 2024
text: Estuarine seine (mock). Juvenile counts: Japanese seabass (Lateolabrax japonicus)=74, Flathead grey mullet (Mugil cephalus)=120, Japanese anchovy (Engraulis japonicus)=510. Notes: low salinity plume; visibility poor; juveniles concentrated at tide change.

title: Seto Inland Sea Bream Fishery Log – Apr 2024
text: Small-scale fishery log (mock). Landed counts: Red sea bream (Pagrus major)=86, Black sea bream (Acanthopagrus schlegelii)=44. Size notes: Pagrus major total length 28–51 cm. Notes: clear water; mild current; bait effectiveness higher near reef edges.

title: Onagawa Acoustic Transect – Apr 2024 (Spring Pulse)
text: Acoustic survey (mock, 120 kHz). NASC index: surface layer=1,120; midwater=310. Paired net samples: Engraulis japonicus high; Trachurus japonicus medium. Notes: spring increase in pelagic biomass index; patchy distribution.

title: Onagawa Trawl Survey – Apr 2024 (Week 3)
text: Trawl survey summary (mock). Total catch counts: Japanese anchovy (Engraulis japonicus)=780, Japanese jack mackerel (Trachurus japonicus)=220, Chub mackerel (Scomber japonicus)=140, Rockfish (Sebastes spp)=18. Notes: anchovy surge consistent with acoustic signal; sea state calm.

title: Onagawa eDNA Snapshot – Apr 2024 (Spring)
text: eDNA metabarcoding (mock). Relative read abundance: Engraulis japonicus very high, Trachurus japonicus medium, Sardinops melanostictus low. Notes: spring pelagic signature; interpret reads cautiously (PCR bias possible).

title: Sanriku Offshore Trawl – Jun 2024 (Shelf Edge)
text: Offshore trawl (mock). Total catch counts: Chub mackerel (Scomber japonicus)=360, Skipjack tuna (Katsuwonus pelamis)=40, Pacific saury (Cololabis saira)=25. Notes: mixed pelagic assemblage; SST 19.3C; moderate swell.

title: Onagawa Hypoxia Check – Aug 2024 (Fish Risk)
text: Water column profile (mock). Bottom dissolved oxygen: 3.2–4.1 mg/L in inner bay; SST 25.8C. Fish observations: anchovy schools remained surface; demersal catches reduced. Notes: mild hypoxia risk; advise avoiding prolonged bottom sets in inner bay.

title: Onagawa Bottom Trawl – Aug 2024 (Demersal Shift)
text: Bottom trawl (mock). Total catch counts: Olive flounder (Paralichthys olivaceus)=11, Rockfish (Sebastes spp)=22, Pacific cod (Gadus macrocephalus)=6. Notes: lower demersal abundance coincident with low bottom oxygen; surface pelagics seen visually.

title: Sendai Bay Sardine Boom – Aug 2024 (Purse Seine)
text: Purse seine summary (mock). Total catch (kg): Japanese sardine (Sardinops melanostictus)=14,600, Chub mackerel (Scomber japonicus)=1,980. Notes: sardine-dominated sets; tight schools; minimal bycatch.

title: Ishinomaki Market Landings – Aug 2024 (Daily)
text: Market landing report (mock). Landed weight (t): Sardinops melanostictus=9.8, Scomber japonicus=3.6, Trachurus japonicus=1.9. Notes: sardine size grade improved; higher yield per basket reported by handlers.

title: Onagawa Tagging Release – Sep 2024 (Amberjack)
text: Tagging summary (mock). Japanese amberjack (Seriola quinqueradiata) tagged and released: n=42. Size range: 41–68 cm fork length. Notes: release survival high; tags configured for 15-min depth/temp logging; initial post-release depth 5–18 m.

title: Onagawa Recapture Report – Oct 2024 (Amberjack)
text: Recapture note (mock). Tagged Seriola quinqueradiata recaptured: n=3 after 21–33 days at liberty. Growth: +1.2 to +2.8 cm. Notes: movement remained coastal; temperature log shows diel vertical shifts (surface at dawn, deeper mid-day).

title: Sanriku Salmon Coastal Index – Nov 2024
text: Coastal index (mock). Returning chum salmon (Oncorhynchus keta) counts at monitoring weir: 2,140 (week total). Notes: peak migration aligned with first major cooling event; river temp dropped from 14.0C to 11.6C.

title: Hokkaido Pollock Trawl – Jan 2025 (Winter)
text: Trawl summary (mock). Total catch counts: Walleye pollock (Gadus chalcogrammus)=520, Pacific cod (Gadus macrocephalus)=34. Notes: pollock dominated; size mode 33–38 cm; strong currents; cold surface air.

title: Onagawa Trawl Survey – Mar 2025 (Pre-spring)
text: Trawl survey summary (mock). Total catch counts: Japanese anchovy (Engraulis japonicus)=210, Japanese sardine (Sardinops melanostictus)=95, Rockfish (Sebastes spp)=40. Notes: pelagic schools patchy; increasing daylight; water temp 9.8–10.6C.

title: Onagawa Acoustic Transect – Mar 2025 (Line B)
text: Acoustic survey (mock, 70 kHz). NASC index: surface=540; midwater=190. Ground-truth trawl: Engraulis japonicus medium; Sardinops melanostictus low. Notes: weaker scattering than April 2024; likely pre-spring baseline.

title: Onagawa Set Net CPUE Log – May 2025 (Daily)
text: Set net log (mock). CPUE (kg/net/day): Trachurus japonicus=52.1, Scomber japonicus=31.4, Seriola quinqueradiata=18.7. Notes: jack mackerel increased with warming; short-lived baitfish pulse observed.

title: Sendai Bay Anchovy Event – May 2025
text: Nearshore pelagic event (mock). Visual school estimate + sample haul: Engraulis japonicus count=1,900 (sampled), extrapolated local school density high. Notes: seabird feeding activity elevated; water clarity high; light wind.

title: Onagawa eDNA Snapshot – May 2025 (Pelagic Mix)
text: eDNA metabarcoding (mock). Relative read abundance: Engraulis japonicus high, Trachurus japonicus medium, Scomber japonicus medium. Notes: mixed pelagic composition; consistent with set net CPUE.

title: Sanriku Demersal Index – Jun 2025 (Bottom Longline)
text: Bottom longline (mock). Hooked counts: Pacific cod (Gadus macrocephalus)=19, Rockfish (Sebastes spp)=46, Olive flounder (Paralichthys olivaceus)=7. Notes: higher rockfish on rough bottom sets; bait loss increased due to small fish.

title: Onagawa Trawl Survey – Jul 2025 (Week 2)
text: Trawl survey summary (mock). Total catch counts: Japanese anchovy (Engraulis japonicus)=690, Chub mackerel (Scomber japonicus)=410, Japanese sardine (Sardinops melanostictus)=170. Notes: anchovy dominant; drizzle early; visibility improved by noon.

title: Ishinomaki Market Landings – Jul 2025 (Weekly)
text: Market landing report (mock). Landed weight (t): Engraulis japonicus=18.3, Scomber japonicus=22.7, Sardinops melanostictus=9.4. Notes: mackerel grades split (small/large); higher demand for larger size class.

title: Onagawa Size Composition – Jul 2025 (Anchovy)
text: Biological sampling (mock). Engraulis japonicus length-frequency: mode=10.5–11.5 cm; secondary mode=13.0–14.0 cm (n=250). Notes: two cohorts inferred; maturity staging suggests older cohort nearing spawning condition.

title: Onagawa Mackerel Condition Index – Aug 2025
text: Condition sampling (mock). Scomber japonicus Fulton’s K: mean=0.98 (SD=0.07, n=80). Notes: moderate condition; stomach contents dominated by small pelagic fish (likely anchovy) and copepods; surface feeding observed at dusk.

title: Sendai Bay Saury Return – Sep 2025
text: Pelagic survey (mock). Cololabis saira catch counts: 210; Scomber japonicus: 180; Engraulis japonicus: 75. Notes: saury presence increased vs August; cooler surface water (19–20C) noted.

title: Onagawa Environmental Log – Sep 2025 (Transition)
text: Environmental log (mock). SST: 20–22C; wind: moderate; visibility: variable with intermittent drizzle. Notes: pelagic assemblage shifting toward saury + mackerel; anchovy schools less persistent nearshore.

title: Kesennuma Tuna Surface Schooling – Sep 2025 (Observation)
text: Surface observation + sample (mock). Skipjack tuna (Katsuwonus pelamis) estimated school size: 1,000–3,000 individuals; sample hooked count=23. Notes: birds actively foraging; baitfish likely anchovy present; SST 22.4C.

title: Onagawa Bottom Trawl – Oct 2025 (Cod Uptick)
text: Bottom trawl (mock). Total catch counts: Pacific cod (Gadus macrocephalus)=41, Rockfish (Sebastes spp)=52, Olive flounder (Paralichthys olivaceus)=14. Notes: cod share increased with cooling; bottom temp 13.1C.

title: Sanriku Herring Coastal Check – Dec 2025
text: Coastal check (mock). Pacific herring (Clupea pallasii) counts in test haul: 640; sand lance (Ammodytes personatus): 310. Notes: winter schooling detected near shore; cold clear water; low wind.

title: Onagawa Annual Summary – 2023 (Fish-Only Highlights)
text: Annual summary (mock). Dominant pelagics: Engraulis japonicus peak in Aug–Sep; Scomber japonicus stable through summer; Cololabis saira increased in Oct. Demersals: Gadus macrocephalus and Sebastes spp increased in late autumn. Notes: drizzle/visibility variability noted in multiple August logs.

title: Onagawa Annual Summary – 2024 (Fish-Only Highlights)
text: Annual summary (mock). Spring pulse: anchovy + jack mackerel increased April–May (acoustic + trawl alignment). Summer: sardine boom regionally (Sendai Bay); inner-bay low bottom oxygen periods corresponded with reduced demersal catches. Autumn: amberjack tagging indicated coastal residency for part of season.

title: Onagawa Annual Summary – 2025 (Fish-Only Highlights)
text: Annual summary (mock). Summer 2025 anchovy dominated multiple trawls; mackerel condition moderate. Autumn cooling coincided with higher cod + rockfish in bottom trawls. Notes: fish community shows recurring seasonal structure with interannual variability in saury timing.

title: Sendai Bay Market Price Notes – Aug 2024 (Sardine)
text: Market note (mock). Sardinops melanostictus: high volume week; price stabilized after initial spike. Size grade: 16–19 cm preferred for processing. Notes: quality complaints decreased as handling time shortened.

title: Onagawa Bycatch Note – Sep 2023 (Trawl)
text: Bycatch note (mock). Non-target fish in pelagic trawl: Sebastes spp=12, Paralichthys olivaceus=3, Gadus macrocephalus=1. Notes: bycatch rate low; recommend maintaining tow depth above 30 m to reduce demersal capture.

title: Onagawa Gear Metadata – Aug 2023 (Pelagic Trawl)
text: Gear metadata (mock). Pelagic trawl: mouth width 18 m, codend mesh 20 mm; tow speed 3.0 kn; tow duration 25 min; target depth 10–25 m. Notes: suited for anchovy/mackerel schooling layer.

title: Onagawa QA/QC Note – eDNA vs Trawl – Apr 2024
text: QA/QC note (mock). Alignment check: eDNA (Engraulis high) matched trawl (anchovy high) and acoustic (surface NASC elevated). Divergence: low sardine reads despite occasional sardine in nets. Notes: potential primer bias or spatial mismatch in sampling.

title: Onagawa Fish Health Check – Aug 2025 (Mackerel)
text: Fish health note (mock). Scomber japonicus external exam: minor skin lesions in 6/90 fish; gill condition normal; parasite presence moderate (visual). Notes: no mass mortality observed; advise continued monitoring during warm SST periods.

title: Sanriku Reef-Edge Survey – Jul 2024 (Rockfish)
text: Reef-edge sampling (mock). Sebastes spp counts: 210 (combined sets), size range 12–29 cm. Notes: higher abundance near complex habitat; minimal pelagic presence in these sets; clear water.

title: Onagawa Trawl Survey – Dec 2023 (Early Winter)
text: Trawl survey summary (mock). Total catch counts: Gadus macrocephalus=35, Sebastes spp=61, Paralichthys olivaceus=9. Notes: pelagics sparse; bottom temp 9.6C; short daylight; calm seas.

title: Onagawa QA Note – Effort Standardization (Trawl)
text: Method note (mock). Standardized tow: 25 min at 3.0 kn; target depth 10–25 m for pelagics. Reporting: counts + CPUE (count/tow) recommended for comparing weeks. Notes: effort variability can bias apparent abundance trends.
